name: Cooked Quinoa or Teff
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 4
1. Measure quinoa into a saucepan half full of cold water.
2. Swish the quinoa briskly with your hand, then pour it through a fine strainer to drain. Repeat two or more times until the water runs clear and doesn't foam when agitated.
3. Drain again.
4. Combine quinoa (or teff), water, and salt in the saucepan. Bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 15 to 20 minutes, until the water is absorbed.
6. Remove from heat and cover for an additional 5 minutes.
Note: Add cooked quinoa or teff to soups either:
1. Uncooked, about half an hour before serving time
2. Cooked (leftover), about 10 minutes before serving time
(If frozen, allow 20 minutes.)
